content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a modular "skeleton" to unify fragmented LLM self-reflection mechanisms: multiple perspective reflection methods like Self-Contrast and Mirror can be plugged into Self-Refine's iterative feedback loop to create a hybrid system that retains Self-Refine's zero extra training advantage while fixing its limitation of single-perspective feedback blindness. This hybrid framework would enable LLMs to refine outputs across all task types (code generation, reasoning, open-ended writing) without supervised data, by leveraging diverse inconsistent self-critiques at each iteration.
